scope: Spec verification plan VP1 + VP2 (phase p1 — shared-transport unit tests)

steps.actual: 14 pass / 0 fail / 61 expect() calls — includes overflow drop+gap sentinel + depth metric
steps.expected: All BufferedChannel unit tests pass (overflow at 15000 events, gap sentinel at correct seq range, depth metric)

steps.actual: Literal `bun test` fails: MockWebSocket stubbing uses vi.stubGlobal/vi.unstubAllGlobals which Bun's vitest-compat does not implement. Re-ran via vitest (the runner declared in packages/shared-transport/package.json `test` script): 8 pass / 0 fail — includes backoff sequence [1000,3000,9000,27000,30000x6], backoff reset after 10s healthy, collision replace, stop() prevents reconnect, structured reconnect/established/dropped logs.
steps.notes: Spec drift: VP2 command string says `bun test` but the test file imports from 'vitest' and uses vitest-only APIs (vi.stubGlobal, vi.fn().mock.calls). The authoritative runner per package.json is vitest. Recommend updating the VP in planning/specs/1-session-runner-decoupling.md to `pnpm --filter @duraclaw/shared-transport test` (or delete the bun test command).

summary: Phase p1 unit tests (VP1 + VP2) all pass. VP2 command in spec is wrong (bun test) — must run via vitest.
